data.title: Lost Money on Neogen Corporation (NEOG)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before September 16, 2025 to Join Class Action
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Neogen Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160552&wire=1&utm_campaign=12

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Neogen Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between January 5, 2023 and June 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants issued a series of materially false and misleading statements which led investors to believe that the integration with the Food Safety Division of the 3M Company was progressing much better than it actually was. In addition, even when the Company was forced to reveal that certain "inefficiencies" arose as a result of the integration, defendants downplayed them and assured investors that they were fully aware and committed to resolving them quickly.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Neogen Corporation stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160552&wire=1&utm_campaign=12 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

data.title: Gilead Sciences Is Maintained at Sector Perform by RBC Capital
Sylvamo Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sylvamo Corp, a global leader in uncoated paper and pulp products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including decreased sales and increased costs across various segments and geographies.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $794 million, reflecting a decrease from $933 million in the prior year, primarily due to lower volumes in North America and decreased sales price and mix in Europe.

Income Before Income Taxes: $20 million, compared to $113 million in the previous year, impacted by lower sales and higher planned maintenance outage costs.

Net Income: $15 million, down from $83 million in the second quarter of 2024, influenced by decreased sales and increased costs.

Earnings Per Share - Diluted: $0.37, compared to $1.98 in the prior year, reflecting the decline in net income.

Adjusted EBITDA: $82 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 10%, compared to $164 million and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18% in the second quarter of 2024, indicating reduced profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Europe: The segment experienced a decrease in sales by $25 million in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, primarily due to lower sales price and mix, and lower volumes, partially offset by favorable foreign exchange impacts.
  • Latin America: The segment saw a $38 million decrease in sales in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, driven by lower volumes, a decrease in sale price and mix, and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts.
  • North America: Sales decreased by $74 million in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, primarily due to a decrease in volumes, partially offset by an increase in sales price and mix.

Geographical Performance

  • Europe: Operating profit was $46 million lower in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, driven by lower sales price and mix, higher planned maintenance outages, and higher input costs.
  • Latin America: Operating profit decreased by $35 million in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, primarily due to lower sales price and mix, higher planned maintenance outages, and higher operating costs.
  • North America: Operating profit was $11 million lower in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024, primarily due to lower volumes and higher input costs, despite higher sales price and mix.

Future Outlook

For Q3 2025, the company expects unfavorable price and mix primarily driven by paper and pulp prices in Europe, with favorable volume due to stronger seasonality in Latin America and North America. Operations and costs are projected to improve slightly, with stable input and transportation costs and no planned maintenance outages.

Operational Challenges

The company faced higher planned maintenance outage costs in Q2 2025 due to complex outages in five mills, impacting operational performance.

Operational Strategy

The company continued to return cash to shareholders through an $18 million dividend payment and repurchased $20 million in shares during Q2 2025.

SEC Filing: Sylvamo Corp [ SLVM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: HANMI FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Hanmi Financial Corp, a prominent player in the financial services industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market positioning.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $101.3 million, reflecting an increase from $98.7 million in the prior year period, driven by higher interest on loans and securities.
  • Net Interest Income Before Credit Loss Expense: $57.1 million, up from $48.6 million, indicating improved interest margins.
  • Net Income: $15.1 million, compared to $14.5 million in the previous year, showing a positive growth in profitability.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share (EPS): $0.50, up from $0.48, reflecting the company's improved earnings performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.50, consistent with Basic EPS, indicating minimal dilution impact.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from various segments, including interest and fees on loans receivable, interest on securities, and dividends on FHLB stock. The interest and fees on loans receivable were the largest contributor to revenue, indicating strong performance in the lending segment.
  • Sales Units: The company originated and transferred $108.6 million in loans held for sale during the six months ended June 30, 2025, indicating robust loan origination activity.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in loan origination and servicing activities, supported by a stable economic environment and effective risk management strategies. The company also expects to maintain its strong capital position, which will support future business expansion and shareholder returns.

SEC Filing: HANMI FINANCIAL CORP [ HAFC ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: GENTEX CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Gentex Corporation, a leading supplier of digital vision, connected car products, and premium audio equipment, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, driven by strategic acquisitions and product innovations.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $657.9 million, representing a 15% increase compared to the second quarter of 2024, primarily driven by the acquisition of VOXX.

Gross Profit: $225.3 million, with an improved gross margin due to purchasing cost reductions, an improved product mix, and operational efficiencies.

Income from Operations: $118.5 million, reflecting higher sales and improved gross margin.

Net Income Attributable to Gentex Corporation: $96.0 million, up 12% from the second quarter of 2024, supported by gross margin expansion.

Earnings Per Share - Diluted: $0.43, compared to $0.37 in the second quarter of 2024, indicating improved profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company's revenue is divided into three main segments: Automotive Products, Premium Audio Products, and Other. Automotive Products include digital vision and connected car products, Premium Audio Products consist of high-quality audio equipment, and the Other segment includes Fire Protection, Dimmable Aircraft Windows, Nanofiber, Medical, Aftermarket, Consumer Electronics, and Biometrics.

Geographical Performance: The Automotive Products segment saw significant sales in the U.S., Japan, Germany, Korea, Mexico, and China. Notably, sales in Japan increased, while sales in China decreased compared to the previous year.

Sales Units: The company reported a 5% decrease in total auto-dimming mirror unit shipments for the second quarter of 2025, with a notable 13% decrease in exterior auto-dimming mirror unit shipments. Interior auto-dimming mirror unit shipments remained relatively flat.

New Product Launches: The company launched the eSight Go product line, which began shipments during the third quarter of 2024, contributing to sales in the medical devices category.

Operational Efficiency: The company achieved a decrease in the cost of goods sold as a percentage of net sales, improving gross margins due to purchasing cost reductions, improved product mix, and operational efficiencies.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued integration and expansion of VOXX's product lines, which are expected to complement existing businesses and expand the company's reach in the consumer technology and connected home space.

SEC Filing: GENTEX CORP [ GNTX ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: ProFrac Holding Corp reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• ProFrac Holding Corp reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 56 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -2 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 25 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -30 cents to -20 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 13.4% to $501.90 million from a year ago; analysts expected $512.67 million.

  • ProFrac Holding Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 67 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $105.9 million.

  • ProFrac Holding Corp shares had fallen by 21.1% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

data.title: Energy firm Orsted said to weigh up to 5 billion euros rights issue, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Danish energy firm Orsted is considering raising as much as 5 billion euros ($5.83 billion) from a rights offering to shore up its finances, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The fundraising plan could be announced in the coming weeks if Orsted decides to proceed, the report said.

Reuters could not immediately confirm the report.

($1 = 0.8576 euros)

data.title: Fannie, Freddie Shares Jump as Trump Pushes for Stock Offering This Year — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Light

President Donald Trump is considering holding a public offering for mortgage giants Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac as soon as this year. The details will be critical for shareholders, mortgage borrowers, and holders of the companies' mortgage bonds.

Trump over the past few weeks met with the chief executives of banks including Citigroup, Bank of America, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan Chase to discuss a potential stock offering of Fannie and Freddie, according to a senior administration official. The offering could be held as soon as this year, and "the president is weighing all his options," the official said.

Shares of Fannie Mae rose about 18% to $9.79 as of midday Friday, and Freddie Mac rose 21% to $7.99, after The Wall Street Journal earlier reported Trump was considering the move.

Fannie and Freddie don't make mortgages themselves. They buy them from lenders, wrap them into mortgage-backed securities, and make guarantees to make bond investors whole if borrowers default. The companies failed during the 2008 financial crisis, and the government put them into a so-called conservatorship managed by the Federal Housing Finance Agency. Since then, the White House and lawmakers have tried and failed multiple times to find a way to bring the companies out of government control.

The FHFA and Treasury Department didn't respond to requests for comment.

Releasing Fannie and Freddie from government control has been a long-stated goal for Trump. The president tried during his first term, but ultimately ran out of time as the White House turned its focus to the Covid-19 pandemic.

Trump reignited interest in a potential Fannie and Freddie offering in May, posting on social media that he was working on taking the companies public. Since then, however, agency officials have revealed scant detail about what such an offering could mean.

A primary point of confusion has been assertions from both Trump and FHFA Director Bill Pulte that the president would sell shares in the companies but still keep them in conservatorship or under government control.

An offering this year would be a much more aggressive timeline than most analysts have expected.

The potential offering would seek to raise around $30 billion, involve the government selling shares, and value the companies at about $500 billion combined, the The Wall Street Journal reported and the senior administration official confirmed.

It's as yet unclear how the administration would treat the current holders of Fannie and Freddie's legacy stocks. Under the terms of the bailout agreement, the government also owns warrants to acquire nearly 80% of the companies' common stock and hundreds of billions of dollars of "senior" preferred shares. What the government does with those stakes will be critical to how much money current shareholders would make in a public offering. During the first Trump administration, officials considered converting the government's senior preferred shares into common stock, a move that would have severely diluted existing private shareholders.

The White House will also need to weigh concerns from the companies' bond investors, some of whom have warned that mortgage rates could rise depending on whether and how the companies exit conservatorship.

The companies' bailout agreements include a government backstop in case the companies run out of capital, a shield that the companies currently don't pay for and that would have to be dealt with if the companies left U.S. control.

Trump has posted on social media that the companies would retain the "implicit GUARANTEES" of the government. That post apparently referred to the pre-crisis situation of the companies, when investors assumed the government would bail them out in a crisis even though the government and companies explicitly disavowed that would happen.

Pulte told Barron's in July that Fannie and Freddie would likely remain in conservatorship under Trump, and some investors have questioned whether a public offering could be successful if the president is unwilling to cede control of the companies.

Write to Joe Light at joe.light@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Heartflow Stock Opens Nearly 50% Above IPO Price in Latest Hot Debut — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Paul R. La Monica

Another day and another hearty debut for an initial public offering on Wall Street.

Medical imaging and diagnostics software company Heartflow is Friday's hot IPO — and the company, which raised $317 million from the sale of 16.7 million shares at a price of $19, popped in early trading.

Shares, which are listed under the ticker symbol of HTFL on the Nasdaq Stock Market, opened at $28, nearly 50% above their offering price. The company is worth about $2.5 billion at Friday's opening price. Bain Capital and Wellington Management are top shareholders.

Heartflow is not profitable, and losses widened in both 2024 and the first quarter of 2025 from year-earlier periods. But sales have grown at a solid clip, rising 44% last year and 39% in the first three months of 2025.

The first-day gain for Heartflow is yet another sign of the renewed interest in IPOs among traders. Shares of Firefly Aerospace, a space exploration company that successfully had a lunar lander touch down on the moon earlier this year, rose more than 35% in its debut Thursday.

Design software firm Figma, drone maker AIRO, and stablecoin company Circle Internet Group, which is set to report earnings on Tuesday Aug. 12, have also wowed Wall Street. Exchange operators Bullish and Miami International are on tap to begin trading next week.

Write to Paul R. La Monica at paul.lamonica@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Shareholders of Fiserv, Inc. (FI): Protect Your Rights Before September 22, 2025 - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Fiserv, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/fiserv-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160553&wire=1&utm_campaign=21

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Fiserv, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between July 24, 2024 and July 22, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (a) Due to cost issues and other problems with its older point-of-sale platform, Payeezy, Fiserv forced Payeezy merchants to convert to its Clover platform; (b) Clover's revenue growth and GPV growth were temporarily boosted by these conversions, which concealed a slowdown in new merchant business; (c) shortly after these conversions, a significant portion of former Payeezy merchants switched to competing solutions due to Clover's high pricing, inadequate customer service, and other issues; (d) as a result of these merchant losses, Clover's GPV growth was significantly slowing, and its revenue growth was unsustainable; and (e) based on the foregoing, Fiserv's positive class period statements about Clover growth strategies, competition, attrition, GPV growth, and business prospects were materially false and misleading.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Fiserv, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/fiserv-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160553&wire=1&utm_campaign=21 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: U.S. Oil Rig Count Hit Brakes on 14-Week Decline — Market Talk
data.description:
1345 ET [Dow Jones]--The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up by one this week to 411, ending a string of 14 weeks of declines, oil service firm Baker Hughes reports. Oil rigs were down by 74 from a year ago. Natural-gas rigs slipped by one to 123 after adding 16 in the previous three weeks. Bank of America strategists Francisco Blanch and Clifton White attribute the climb in natural-gas rigs, led by the Haynesville region, partly to optimism about demand for LNG exports. "It only took 2026 gas prices trading $4-$4.5/mmBtu year to date to increase the natural-gas rig count by more than 20% since 1Q25," they said in a note. "We expect to see incremental production from these newly added gas rigs just as Golden Pass [LNG] enters service by the end of the year." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1345 ET - Local governments in the U.S. have been flooding the market with new municipal bonds. The added supply has been blamed for a decline in the price --and consequent increase in yields-- of munis. Painful as they may be for bond holders, high yields are an appealing entry point for investors interested in the munis' tax exemption benefit. Conning's Cindy Beualieu expects munis to remain attractive, but at more normal levels. Issuance, which reached a record $513.6 billion last year and is 18% higher year-to-date from a year earlier, is likely to settle around $200 billion a year, "which is reasonable" she says. "We aren't calling for significantly lower rates." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1342 ET - A Fed cut in September could normalize the Treasurys yield curve, Cunning's Cindy Beaulieu says. Yields are getting higher as maturities get longer, starting with the three-year Treasurys, but not in the front-end of the curve, where shorter maturities are paying more. The anomaly will correct itself with a Fed cut, Beaulieu says. A normal, steepening shape "is positive from the perspective of building income," something appealing to institutional investors such as insurance companies and some pension plans, Beaulieu says. Long-term yields --or 10- to 30-year-- are likely to remain pressured upward as markets worry about budget deficits and how they will be funded, she says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1340 ET - The Fed faces a thorny dilemma next month, with inflation still running above target while labor indicators weaken. Inflation "is going to be an incredible challenge," Conning's Cindy Beaulieu says. She expects tariffs to push inflation higher for a few months, but "that should be transitory." Meanwhile, inflation expectations remain anchored, despite recent increases, she says. Employment, however, could pave the way for a rate cut. Labor data so far, plus August payrolls to be released before the Fed's next meeting, "will probably give them enough cover to cut 25 basis points." Beaulieu thinks the Fed could leave the door open to pause again after that. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1227 ET - U.S. apartment rent growth continued to slow throughout July, with the average holding at $1,717, Apartments.com says. The month-over-month change was a negligible 0.03% lower, marking the sixth straight month of flat or negative growth. Annual rent growth has also slowed, from 1.5% in January to 1.1% in July. The downward trajectory reflects persistent supply pressures and a cooling demand environment. However, the pace of moderation remains measured, and the national average is still above year-ago levels. Market-level performance diverged in July, with more than half of the nation's 50 largest apartment markets by inventory posting month-over-month rent declines. This prolonged shift could weigh on annual growth figures in the months ahead. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1043 ET - Monster Beverage is benefiting from broad-based strength in the energy drink category, fueled by price convergence with carbonated soft drinks, rising demand for functional beverages and cooler space gains, UBS analysts say in a research note. The company has warned of some tariff-driven pressures in the coming quarters--largely stemming from higher aluminum prices--though mitigation efforts and price increases should cushion this impact. Monster says that pricing actions will be "surgical and dependent on packaging and channel," rather than broad-based across the portfolio. The analysts say that Monster's momentum and fundamental trajectory is favorable, though they note that these factors are likely already priced into the stock. Shares climb 7.8%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1036 ET - Wendy's cut its 2025 guidance for the second time this year as U.S. sales slump, but the stock isn't getting fried as the burger chain reports pockets of strength elsewhere. The company's profit ticked up to $55.1 million from $54.6 million and adjusted earnings of 29 cents a share topped analyst forecasts by 4 cents, according to FactSet. The company's operating profit was boosted by lower advertising spending, reorganization and realignment costs and overheads. Wendy's also highlighted that its international business delivered 8.7% higher systemwide sales during the quarter. Shares rise 1% to $10.05. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1030 ET - Expedia's stock is set to see the most upside among travel competitors if trends in the U.S. accelerate, Oppenheimer analysts say in a note. Expedia has greater exposure to the U.S. compared to peers like Booking and Airbnb, and management noted broad-based improvement in U.S. travel demand in July, though the low-end consumer is still challenged. Expedia's stock "offers most torque to improving US travel environment," the analysts say, bringing their price target to $250, from $210. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1024 ET - Truist analysts say they aren't surprised Monster Beverage shares are trading higher after the energy-drink maker posted 2Q beats on both revenue and earnings. "The 2Q topline beat looks strong at first glance," they write, but note U.S. sales are still benefiting from a 5% price increase taken in November. Growth has also slowed. The top line is up about 14% over the past two years, a pace the company used to achieve in just one, the analysts write. Gross margins could come under pressure from tariffs, and Monster is weighing another price increase for later this year. Shares rise 7.1%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0951 ET - Expedia's raised guidance and commentary around an uptick in overall travel demand, particularly in the U.S., provided a powerful combination to send investors into euphoria, Benchmark analysts say in a note. Other travel players have similarly touted improved travel trends, but warned of tougher comparisons in the current quarter or persisting pressures going forward, the analysts say. "Rarely have we seen such enthusiasm for a 1% gross booking/revenue and 25bp margin increase in annual guidance," the analysts say, "But we all know that the story is just as important as the journey." Shares rise 3.5%, to $194.46. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

0940 ET - Staar Surgical's largest shareholder is raising questions about the implantable-lens maker's $1.5 billion deal to be acquired by Swiss eye-care giant Alcon. New York private investment firm Broadwood Capital, which owns more than 27% of Staar, says in an SEC filing that it hasn't decided whether it will support the buyout and that it wants to see documents relating to the process that led to the deal. Broadwood says it also plans to explore alternatives to the deal, including through contact with possible other buyers. Staar up 1% to $27.21, shy of the $28/share takeover price. (colin.kellaher@wsj.com)

0919 ET - Sweetgreen's softer results will likely continue in the near-term as traffic gets weighed down by industry-wide pressures and consumers' increasing focus on value, KeyBanc Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Sweetgreen could see some stabilizing or improving trends as brand-specific headwinds, like the transition of its loyalty program, begin to subside, and as tactics like portion sizing and better operational execution start to bear fruit, the analysts note. But overall, the company is set up for only modest sequential improvement in the rest of the year as near-term uncertainty continues, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)
